value.regime_read: NQ weak vs ES — rotation OUT of tech. A breadth/rotation warning; tech-led longs are suspect.
value.narrative:
Macro backdrop —
[2026-07-17] US economic state (FRED):
  Growth: real GDP 2.1% (q/q ann)
  Inflation: CPI 3.23% YoY | core CPI 2.25% | core PCE n/a (Fed target 2%)
  Labor: unemployment 4.2%
  Rates: fed funds 3.63% | 10Y 4.55% | 10Y-2Y 0.41% (NORMAL)
  Consumer: UMich sentiment 44.8 | VIX 15.67

---
PRE-US-OPEN BRIEFING — ES/NQ (research only; no execution path, CME futures vs OANDA forex). Scorecard caveat up front: n=13 dir-calls, hit rate 0.769 but regime accuracy only 0.258 — this system is still CALIBRATING. Treat everything below as intelligence, not a validated edge.

1) RECENT MOVE-BY-MOVE READ. Both indices are red and NQ is leading the weakness. ES is -0.9% on the day / -1.45% on the week; NQ is -1.88% on the day / -4.51% on the week. That asymmetry is the whole story this morning: tech is bleeding roughly 2x the broad index. The likely catalyst is right there in the tape — headlines flag 'US stock futures mixed as chip weakness weighs' and a Microsoft/OpenAI/Google AI-arms-race story, i.e. semis and mega-cap AI names taking the hit. Layered underneath is a geopolitical risk premium: Iran/Houthis threatening the Red Sea (Bab al-Mandeb), a drone hitting a tanker at Basra, and produce-price/inflation chatter. None of that is directly bullish for high-multiple tech, and it keeps a bid under an energy/inflation narrative that hurts NQ more than ES. ES sitting almost exactly on its session VWAP (last 7509.75 vs VWAP 7510.43) says the broad tape is balanced/indecisive; NQ trading below its VWAP (28677 vs 28732) confirms tech is the underperformer intraday.

2) VOLUME-PROFILE READ (WHERE volume traded — this is volume-at-price, NOT order-flow delta; true buy/sell aggression needs a CME feed). ES: 5-day POC 7589.55, value area 7569.4–7616.41, high-volume nodes clustered 7586–7607. Price at 7509.75 is trading BELOW the entire value area and below the low end of the accepted range — ES has fallen out of the week's balance zone and is probing the lower edge of the 5-day range (7493.83). The line in the sand for ES bulls is reclaiming ~7569 (VAL); until then, prior acceptance sits overhead as resistance. NQ: POC 29623.8, value area 29403–29844.6, nodes 29477–29734. NQ at 28677 is FAR below its value area — it has completely vacated the week's fair-value zone and is near the bottom of its 5-day range (28575). That's a distribution/breakdown look, not accumulation. Overhead supply is thick from 29400 up. Bottom line: both are below value, NQ dramatically so — sellers are in control of the recent structure.

3) NQ/ES LEAD-LAG REGIME. Classified ROTATION_WARN. Contemporaneous correlation is high (0.926), so these still move together, but the return spread is negative across every window (1d -0.98%, 5d -0.62%, 20d -0.22%, cumulative -4.4%) — NQ is persistently WEAKER than ES. The lead-lag cross-correlation shows no clean leader (best lag corr only -0.102, leader=NONE), so there's no reliable timing edge from one leading the other. The read: this is rotation OUT of tech / a breadth-vs-concentration warning. Implication — tech-led LONGS are suspect and should be sized down. It does NOT confirm a clean directional short edge either, because the lead-lag matrix is noisy. So: trust reduced, size reduced, and I lean with the weaker horse (NQ) rather than fighting it.

4) EVENT-CALENDAR AWARENESS. FOMC in 12 days (2026-07-29, maintained seed — verify). PCE in ~14 days (7/31, cadence estimate). ISM Manufacturing ~17 days (8/3). NFP ~21 days (8/7). Nothing is imminent enough to freeze positioning this morning, but we are inside the pre-FOMC drift window — start biasing toward smaller size / wider stops as we approach the 29th. The PCE/ISM/NFP dates are cadence ESTIMATES, not confirmed prints, so don't hard-anchor to them. Net: no calendar reason to be aggressive today, and one growing reason (FOMC approaching) to keep risk modest.

5) SYNTHESIS — META-REGIME + NARROWED BIAS. Meta-regime: risk-off, tech-underperformance rotation with a geopolitical/inflation risk premium and no clean lead-lag timing signal. The pieces that ARE aligned: NQ well below value, weakest across all spread windows, chip-weakness catalyst in the news, and price under VWAP. The pieces that keep confidence CAPPED: lead-lag has no true leader (noisy), ES is balanced on VWAP (not confirming a broad-market cascade), the driving news is thematic rather than a hard scheduled catalyst, and our own regime-accuracy scorecard is poor (0.258). So this is a mixed-but-tilted-down picture, not a high-conviction setup.

BIAS: SHORT/fade-the-bounce on NQ relative weakness, ~42% conviction. Prefer selling rallies back toward the vacated value area / VWAP zone (28732 and up toward the 29400 supply shelf) rather than chasing the low. INVALIDATION: a reclaim and hold back above the prior-week/prior-day pivot low of 29078.5 (NQ) neutralizes the breakdown thesis — above that, the short edge is gone and this reverts to NEUTRAL. Deeper invalidation of any bearish idea would be NQ pushing back into its value area above ~29403. Given the noisy lead-lag and calibrating scorecard, keep size small and treat this as a lean, not a mandate.
